subject: Social Science
chapter_name: Challenges of India and Their Remedies
learning_outcomes: Understand the meaning of communalism and list measures to control it
Understand the steps taken to eliminate illiteracy
Give suggestions for improving the status of women
Recall the steps taken by the government to eliminate corruption

key_terms.definition: The division of society on the basis of religion and the development of mutual enmity on that basis
The deep-rooted pride that a particular community has for the region in which they live
Regionalism in different regions within a state
Inciting illegal actions through bribery or any other means
The excess profit earning trend at the cost of general public or consumers
Secretly importing/exporting goods without paying import/export duties
When the majority of the population of a country is in the working age group
The state in which people are unable to meet their minimum needs

mcqs.question: The year in which the Right to Information Act was implemented
mcqs.explanation: The Right to Information Act was implemented in 2005

mcqs.question: The first woman judge of the Supreme Court of India
mcqs.explanation: Fatima Beevi was the first woman judge of the Supreme Court of India

mcqs.question: Article 21A of our Constitution pertains to
mcqs.explanation: Article 21A guarantees the Right to Education for children between 6-14 years

mcqs.question: Dr. D.M. Nanjundappa Committee was formed in order to
mcqs.explanation: The Nanjundappa Committee was formed to address regional imbalances in Karnataka

one_mark_questions.question: What is sub-regionalism?
one_mark_questions.answer: Regionalism in different regions within a state is called sub-regionalism

one_mark_questions.question: What is smuggling?
one_mark_questions.answer: The act of secretly importing and sending goods from foreign countries without paying any import and export duties

one_mark_questions.question: Which are the institutions established to prevent corruption in India?
What is communalism?
one_mark_questions.answer: The division of society on the basis of religion and identification of mutual enmity on that basis

one_mark_questions.question: What is profiteering?
one_mark_questions.answer: Profiteering means the excess profit earning trend at the cost of general public or consumers

three_mark_questions.question: Analyse the measures taken to control corruption.
What are the measures taken to improve the status of women?
answer: Formation of the Department of Women and Child Welfare
Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act (2005)
50% reservation for women in local bodies
33% reservation for women in Lok Sabha and Legislative Assemblies
